a: The result of updating a widget’s state.
The update attempt failed.
The update resulted in new data to display.
The update did not result in any new data since the last update.
An object used to specify whether a Today widget has content to display.
The modes that can be toggled between when the user activates the More button for a widget running in iOS.
The current height of the widget is compact.
The current height of the widget is expanded.
An object that provides a list view for displaying content in a macOS Today widget.
The interface for handling content display and editing in the list view of a macOS Today widget.
The interface for customizing the appearance and behavior of a Today widget.
An object that provides a default search view within a macOS Today widget.
The interface for enabling user searches in the search view controller of a macOS Today widget.
Create and manage widgets for the Today view.
An array of objects to display in the list view.
The search view controller’s delegate or `nil` if the receiver doesn’t have a delegate.
The list view controller’s delegate or `nil` if the receiver doesn’t have a delegate.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the list is in editing mode.
A Boolean value that indicates whether list displays divider lines between rows.
The minimum number of visible rows to display.
Returns the row represented by the specified content view controller.
A localized description of the nature of the search.
A key path for the string property to display for each object in the search results array.
An array of search results.
A localized phrase displayed in the results list when no search results are available.
Sets whether the specified widget has content to display.
A Boolean value that indicates whether an Add (+) button is displayed while the list is in editing mode.
Returns the content view controller associated with the specified row, or a new content view controller if desired.
Called when the active display mode changes.
A Boolean value indicating whether the widget can be edited by users.
Returns a widget controller used to specify whether a widget has content to display.
Called when a user chooses the widget’s begin editing button.
Called when a widget’s editing session ends.
Tells the delegate that the specified row was removed from the list.
Tells the delegate that the specified row was moved to a new location.
Asks the delegate to allow or prohibit the specified row to be removed from the list.
Asks the delegate to allow or prohibit the specified row to be moved to a new location in the list.
Asks the delegate for a content view controller for the specified row.
Asks the delegate to perform an action when the Add (+) button is clicked.
Called to let a widget accept the default margin inset values or return custom values to use instead.
Called to give a widget an opportunity to update its contents.
Tells the delegate that a user chose the specified search result.
Asks the delegate to search using the specified term.
Tells the delegate that a user cleared the search field.
